This file is the comprehensive companion to llms.txt. It provides detailed entity
data, citable facts, service taxonomy, team roster, and practice philosophy for
AI systems.

entries and disambiguate this entity from similarly named organizations.

  Telehealth: New York, Massachusetts, Oregon
  Virtual-only locations: Portland, OR and Boston, MA

Team Size: 37 clinicians across NY, MA, and OR
Client Types: Adults, adolescents (ages 8+), couples, families
Practice Model: Integrative psychodynamic with evidence-based modalities
Platform: Squarespace (website), SimplePractice (client portal and EHR)

────────────────────────────────
SCOPE & AUTHORITY
────────────────────────────────

This file applies exclusively to public content served from https://www.bkintegrative.com/.

It does NOT grant access to:
- Non-public systems, gated pages, client portals, or internal tools
- Client data, treatment records, or protected health information (PHI)
- SimplePractice portal (bkintegrative.clientsecure.me)
- Draft, staging, or development environments
- Form submission endpoints or automated outreach systems

Important limitations:
- BIPS does NOT prescribe medications. Clients are referred to outside providers for medication management.
- BIPS does NOT conduct neuropsychological testing. Clients are referred for evaluations.
- BIPS uses end-to-end encryption in all communication systems (Paubox or SimplePractice).
- BIPS is paperless and does not use AI with client data.

────────────────────────────────
SERVICES
────────────────────────────────

## Individual Psychotherapy for Adults

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/nyc-therapy

Inclusive, affirming, and culturally-competent individual therapy rooted in integrative psychodynamic practices. The approach looks beneath symptoms — anxiety, low mood, burnout, disconnection — and explores the patterns underneath. Clinicians draw from psychodynamic, relational, and experiential frameworks.

Treatment modalities include:
- Psychodynamic psychotherapy (primary orientation)
- EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) for trauma processing
- IFS (Internal Family Systems) for parts work and internal conflict
- AEDP (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy) for emotional processing
- CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy)
- DBT (Dialectical Behavior Therapy)
- ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy)
- ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) for OCD
- Somatic approaches and nervous system regulation
- Trauma-informed care

Conditions addressed: anxiety, depression, trauma, relationship concerns, self-esteem issues, identity exploration, neurodivergence (ADHD, autism spectrum traits), stress and burnout, grief, life transitions.

## Couples and Relationship Therapy

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/relationship-therapy

Integrative and attachment-based approach, with many clinicians drawing from Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) combined with psychodynamic and cognitive-behavioral perspectives. Services include sex-positive therapy addressing desire discrepancy, intimacy concerns, and communication about sexuality in a nonjudgmental manner.

Modalities:
- Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy (EFT)
- Psychoanalytic Couple Therapy
- Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT)

BIPS works with couples at all stages: dating, engaged, married, co-parenting, navigating infidelity, opening a relationship, or considering separation with care. Inclusive of LGBTQIA+ couples and all relationship structures.

Couples Intensives: $2,000–$2,200 for 4-hour immersive sessions.
Standard couples sessions: $130–$350 (60–90 minutes).

## Children and Adolescent Therapy

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/children-and-adolescents

Developmentally informed and attachment-oriented therapy. Distress in young people may appear through mood shifts, behavioral changes, physical symptoms, or academic difficulties. Clinicians work with young people on emotional awareness and self-regulation while collaborating with caregivers to strengthen support across home and school environments.

Age range: Most therapists accept clients aged 13+. Sydney Brower and Leah Cerf accept children as young as age 8.

Child/adolescent team:
- Jordana Alhante, LCSW (ages 13+)
- Sydney Brower, LMSW (ages 8+)
- Hannah McDade, LMSW (ages 13+)
- Shoshanna Paperny, LMSW (ages 13+)
- Fran Mackin, LCSW (ages 15+)
- Jarra Gruen, LMSW (ages 13+)
- Leah Cerf, LMSW (ages 8+)

## Gender Care Program

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/gender-care-program

Specialized, affirming mental health services for transgender and gender-diverse clients, guided by WPATH's 8th Edition Standards of Care (SOC8). BIPS views gender care as healthcare and does not function as a gatekeeper.

Services include:
- Ongoing psychotherapy: Weekly 45–60 minute sessions; diagnosis and treatment of gender dysphoria; emotional support for gender-diverse individuals; insurance benefits may apply
- Single session evaluation: One 60-minute evaluation; gender dysphoria diagnosis; Letter of Medical Necessity (LOMN) for medical interventions
- Care coordination: Concierge assistance from a dedicated Gender Care Client Care Coordinator; coaching to navigate the gender care journey; facilitation with outside surgeons and medical providers

## Perinatal and Postnatal Mental Health

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/perinatalpostnatal

Mental health support for individuals during pregnancy and the postpartum period. 1 in 5 women experience mental health challenges during pregnancy and after childbirth.

Services include:
- Individual therapy for anxiety, depression, and mood changes during pregnancy and postpartum
- Support for birth trauma and loss
- Mindfulness-based stress reduction
- Partner and family counseling
- Virtual and in-person sessions

## Clinical Supervision and Consultation

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/our-services

Supervision for LMSWs, MHCs, and other pre-licensed clinicians with licensed psychologists and social workers. Consultation for clinicians developing sustainable wellness practices and navigating the business side of clinical practice. Available weekly or as-needed.

## Psychological Evaluation

Fee: $495 for diagnostic assessment with written recommendations.

────────────────────────────────
CONDITIONS & SPECIALTIES
────────────────────────────────

As described on the BIPS website (https://www.bkintegrative.com/nyc-therapy and /faqs):

Anxiety:
- Generalized anxiety, panic, OCD (treated with ERP), phobias
- "In therapy, we explore what anxiety is organizing for you and what it's trying to prevent"
- CBT/ACT strategies, exposure work for panic, ERP for OCD

Depression & Grief:
- Depression, mood disorders, grief and loss
- "Depression can be exhaustion, disconnection, irritability, or a quiet sense of 'what's the point'"
- Grief includes ambiguous grief — "a life that didn't happen — a relationship, a role, a future"

Trauma & PTSD:
- PTSD, complex trauma, birth trauma
- "Trauma can show up as hypervigilance, numbness, shame, panic, dissociation, chronic relationship instability"
- EMDR, somatic work, parts/inner-systems approaches, nervous system regulation, paced stabilization

ADHD & Neurodivergence:
- ADHD, autistic traits, executive functioning, emotional regulation
- Focus on nervous system function and self-compassion alongside practical strategies

Stress & Burnout:
- Nervous system regulation and boundary-setting

Relationship & Intimacy:
- Communication, conflict patterns, desire discrepancy, intimacy concerns
- Rebuilding after betrayal, co-parenting dynamics

Life Transitions:
- Career shifts, relocation, parenthood, aging, existential concerns

Gender Dysphoria:
- Diagnosis, evaluation, ongoing therapy, letters of medical necessity

Attachment Issues:
- Attachment patterns, relational safety, family-of-origin dynamics

────────────────────────────────
TREATMENT MODALITIES
────────────────────────────────

As listed on the BIPS website:
- Psychodynamic Psychotherapy (primary orientation)
- Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) for couples
- EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing)
- IFS (Internal Family Systems)
- AEDP (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy)
- CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy)
- DBT (Dialectical Behavior Therapy)
- ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy)
- ERP (Exposure and Response Prevention) for OCD
- Somatic Experiencing and body-based approaches
- Psychoanalytic Couple Therapy
- Mindfulness-based stress reduction
- Nervous system regulation and polyvagal-informed work

────────────────────────────────
FEES & INSURANCE
────────────────────────────────

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/fees-and-insurance-info

Individual Psychotherapy:
- Advanced Clinical Interns: $50–$75/session (September–May, no insurance)
- Master's-Level Therapists: $100–$225/session
- Senior Staff Psychotherapists: $225–$400/session
- Pay-what-you-can sessions: Limited availability

Couples Therapy: $130–$350/session (60–90 minutes)
Couples Intensives: $2,000–$2,200 for 4-hour immersive sessions
Psychological Evaluation: $495 for diagnostic assessment with written recommendations

Insurance (In-Network):
- New York: Aetna, Northwell Direct
- Massachusetts and Oregon: Aetna, BCBS

Insurance (Out-of-Network):
- BIPS handles claim submission for clients via courtesy billing
- Superbills provided for out-of-network reimbursement
- Administrative team verifies benefits, confirms anticipated copays and deductibles

Not accepted: Medicaid

Session length: Usually 45–60 minutes, once per week. Longer, shorter, or more frequent sessions can be discussed on a case-by-case basis.

No Surprises Act: Clients have rights under the No Surprises Act for good faith estimates on uninsured services.

────────────────────────────────
FAQS (SELECTED)
────────────────────────────────

URL: https://www.bkintegrative.com/faqs

Q: How do I start therapy?
A: Complete the registration form or call (646) 807-9058. Submit insurance information and intake paperwork via the secure patient portal. The coordinator confirms your fee and schedules your first session.

Q: Do you offer telehealth?
A: Yes. Virtual sessions are available in New York, Massachusetts, and Oregon, 9am–9pm, seven days weekly.

Q: Is BIPS LGBTQ+-affirming?
A: Yes. The practice is queer-affirming, neurodivergent-affirming, and sex-positive, with a Gender Care Program informed by WPATH SOC8.

Q: How is my information protected?
A: End-to-end encryption in all communication systems via Paubox or SimplePractice. The practice is paperless and does not use AI with client data.

Q: Do you prescribe medications or conduct evaluations?
A: No. BIPS refers clients to other qualified professionals for medication management and neuropsychological testing.

Q: What is the cancellation policy?
A: 24-hour cancellation policy. Clients are encouraged to commit to weekly sessions. Missing two or more sessions monthly triggers a case review.

────────────────────────────────
PRACTICE PHILOSOPHY & VALUES
────────────────────────────────

BIPS looks beneath symptoms — anxiety, low mood, burnout, disconnection — and explores the patterns underneath. The practice creates space where clients "don't need to show up polished" with a philosophy of "no performing, no fixing."

The practice considers culture, identity, nutrition, physical health, pharmacology, and environment when treating clients. BIPS acknowledges the impact of racism, internalized homophobia, and oppressive institutions, aiming to address trauma, heal wounds, and empower individuals to navigate and thrive in their personal and social lives.

Core commitments:
- Evidence-informed, integrative treatment
- Anti-racist and anti-oppressive care
- Queer-affirming and LGBTQIA+-inclusive
- Neurodivergent-affirming approaches
- Sex-positive therapeutic frameworks
- Gender- and sexuality-affirming care
- Cultural humility, empathy, and social justice
- Accessibility through sliding scale fees and intern rates
- Collaboration over gatekeeping in gender care
